chunk_summary: This section discusses the selection of age-appropriate anthropometric charts and diagnostic thresholds for overnutrition and obesity in children, emphasizing WHO standards for under-5s and IAP BMI charts for ages 5–18 in Indian practice.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of accurate chart selection in pediatric obesity diagnosis.
Learn the WHO weight-for-length/height thresholds for children under 5 years.
Apply IAP 2015 BMI charts and thresholds for children aged 5–18 years in Indian practice.
Recognize the risks of mixing chart systems and the need for consistent documentation.
key_facts: For children under 5 years, use WHO weight-for-length (0–24 months) or weight-for-height (24–59 months) charts.
Overweight is defined as > +2 SD and obesity as > +3 SD above the median for under-5s.
For children aged 5–18 years in Indian practice, use IAP 2015 BMI charts with overweight at adult-equivalent BMI 23 and obesity at BMI 27.
Severe obesity in IAP charts is classified as class 2 (120–140% of BMI 27) and class 3 (≥140% of BMI 27).
Avoid mixing percentile-based systems (e.g., AAP) with IAP charts to ensure consistent clinical decisions.
reference_quote: For children younger than 5 years, use WHO weight-for-length (0–24 months) or weight-for-height (24–59 months). Interpret overweight as > +2 SD and obesity as > +3 SD above the median.
document_key_topics: Avoiding charting errors in clinical practice
